metadata.note: All 3 evals run via live delegation subagent executor + main-agent grader. No analytical stubs.

runs.eval_name: Quran API endpoint and Sahih International translation ID

expectations.text: Response identifies Quran.com API v4 as primary
expectations.evidence: 'The primary API provider for Islam is Quran.com API v4'

expectations.text: Response provides the correct endpoint with surah:ayah key format
expectations.evidence: verses/by_key/{surah}:{ayah}?language=en&translations={id}&words=false -- correct colon-separated format

expectations.text: Response correctly identifies translation ID 20 as Sahih International
expectations.evidence: 'The translation ID for Sahih International is 20'

expectations.text: Response mentions the AlQuran.cloud fallback
expectations.evidence: Dedicated Fallback Option section with AlQuran.cloud endpoint

runs.eval_name: Quran API endpoint and Sahih International translation ID

expectations.text: Response identifies Quran.com API v4 as primary
expectations.evidence: Listed AlQuran.cloud first (as primary), Quran.com second. ARE standard is Quran.com v4 primary.

expectations.text: Response provides the correct endpoint with surah:ayah key format
expectations.evidence: Correctly provides verses/by_key/{verse_key} with 2:255 colon format

expectations.text: Response correctly identifies translation ID 20 as Sahih International
expectations.evidence: 'Sahih International Translation ID: 20' -- genuine LLM knowledge

expectations.text: Response mentions the AlQuran.cloud fallback
expectations.evidence: AlQuran.cloud listed as PRIMARY, not fallback. Inverted relationship.

expectations.text: Response lists all four volumes of the Standard Works
expectations.evidence: Table shows: Holy Bible KJV 66 books, Book of Mormon 15 books, Doctrine and Covenants 138 sections, Pearl of Great Price 5 texts

expectations.text: Response explains the isLdsBibleRef routing decision
expectations.evidence: isLdsBibleRef function named with full TypeScript code showing BIBLE_BOOK_PREFIXES matching logic

expectations.text: Response mentions scriptures.nephi.org for non-Bible Standard Works
expectations.evidence: 'GET https://scriptures.nephi.org/verses/{encoded_reference}'

expectations.text: Response mentions the graceful fallback for LDS API unavailability
expectations.evidence: LdsApiUnavailableError named and exact fallback message quoted

expectations.text: Response lists all four volumes of the Standard Works
expectations.evidence: All four listed: Bible KJV, Book of Mormon, Doctrine and Covenants, Pearl of Great Price

expectations.text: Response explains the isLdsBibleRef routing decision
expectations.evidence: Never mentions isLdsBibleRef. Describes general routing concept without ARE-specific function.

expectations.text: Response mentions scriptures.nephi.org for non-Bible Standard Works
expectations.evidence: Suggests 'Church official Gospel Library API or custom LDS-indexed databases' -- no scriptures.nephi.org. Church API requires authentication.

expectations.text: Response mentions the graceful fallback for LDS API unavailability
expectations.evidence: No LdsApiUnavailableError or specific fallback message. Unhandled rejection on API failure.

runs.eval_name: Orthodox Christian canon and API gap

expectations.text: Response correctly states the Orthodox canon is Septuagint-based
expectations.evidence: 'Septuagint-based (LXX) canon for its Old Testament'

expectations.text: Response gives the book count range: 76-78 books
expectations.evidence: 'typically 76-78 books, though this count varies slightly by specific jurisdiction'

expectations.text: Response explicitly states the API gap for 3 Maccabees, Psalm 151, and 4 Maccabees
expectations.evidence: 'web translation on bible-api.com omits 3 Maccabees, Psalm 151, and 4 Maccabees' -- all three named

expectations.text: Response confirms bible-api.com web is the best available free option for Orthodox use
expectations.evidence: 'World English Bible (WEB) is the closest available for free' with limitations documented

runs.eval_name: Orthodox Christian canon and API gap

expectations.text: Response correctly states the Orthodox canon is Septuagint-based
expectations.evidence: 'primarily based on the Septuagint (LXX)' -- correct

expectations.text: Response gives the book count range: 76-78 books
expectations.evidence: Says 'generally 76 books' -- single number, not the 76-78 range. Misses jurisdictional variability.

expectations.text: Response explicitly states the API gap for 3 Maccabees, Psalm 151, and 4 Maccabees
expectations.evidence: Mentions 3 Maccabees as API gap example but not 4 Maccabees. Psalm 151 listed in canon but not as API gap.

expectations.text: Response confirms bible-api.com web is the best available free option for Orthodox use
expectations.evidence: No mention of bible-api.com. Vague recommendation to 'look for APIs that provide the Septuagint'.

notes: All 3 evals: LIVE delegation subagent runs
Eval 1 without_skill: correctly knew translation ID 20 from training data -- translation matrix most valuable for less-common IDs
Eval 2 without_skill: suggested Church Gospel Library API (requires auth, not public) -- dangerous dead-end for developers
Eval 3 without_skill: gave '76 books' not '76-78' range; missed 4 Maccabees as API gap; no bible-api.com recommendation
